text.title: django-quality-queries Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for django-quality-queries as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Using select_related on a nullable ForeignKey and iterating without checking None.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: select_related with a NULL FK produces an outer join and correctly returns None for the related object. Agents sometimes add .exclude(fk__isnull=True) to avoid None checks, which changes the result set semantics.

failure-mode.description: Using prefetch_related but then filtering the related queryset in Python inside the loop.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Filtering in Python after prefetch breaks the optimization — the filter re-issues queries. Use Prefetch(queryset=...) with the filter applied to the queryset argument instead.

failure-mode.description: Adding select_related to every queryset including those that never access the related object.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Unnecessary JOINs add CPU and memory cost. Profile first with Debug Toolbar, add select_related only where the related field is actually accessed.

failure-mode.description: prefetch_related on a queryset that has already been evaluated (e.g., list(queryset)).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: prefetch_related works at queryset evaluation time. Calling it after list() has no effect. Use prefetch_related_objects() for already-evaluated querysets.
